momentum: how strongly and consistently the price has been rising
volume: how many shares are being traded — higher means more people are active
volatility: how much the price jumps up and down day to day
debt_to_equity: how much debt the company carries compared to what it owns
profit_margin: how many cents the company keeps from every dollar it earns
moving_average: the average price over recent weeks — shows the general trend direction
drawdown: how far the stock has fallen from its recent highest price
sector: the industry group this company belongs to
ETF: a fund that tracks an entire sector — used to measure sector health
breadth: how many stocks in a sector are moving in the same direction
regime: the current overall market environment based on fear levels
VIX: the market fear index — higher means more uncertainty overall
relative_strength: how this stock is performing compared to others in its sector
signal_agreement: how many of our indicators are pointing in the same direction
beta: how much the stock tends to move relative to the overall market — higher beta means larger swings
EPS: earnings per share — how much profit the company makes for each share of stock
revenue: the total amount of money the company brought in before any expenses
market_cap: the total value of all company shares — small, mid, or large company size
operating_cash_flow: the actual cash the business generates from its day-to-day operations
P/E_ratio: price-to-earnings — how much investors are paying for each dollar of profit
sector_rotation: money moving from one industry group to another as conditions change
MA50: 50-day moving average — the average price over the past 50 trading days
MA200: 200-day moving average — the average price over the past 200 trading days
risk_adjusted_momentum: how strong the trend is after accounting for how bumpy the ride has been
composite_confidence: our overall assessment combining risk, opportunity, signal alignment, and sector strength
advance_decline: the ratio of stocks rising versus falling — measures broad market participation
risk_score: our estimate of how much risk this company currently carries (lower is safer)
opportunity_score: our estimate of how attractive the current setup looks for research (higher is better)
trailing_stop: the idea of selling if a stock falls a set percentage from its recent high
support_level: a price zone where the stock has historically stopped falling and bounced back
resistance_level: a price zone where the stock has historically struggled to move higher
